rulebook_purpose: Conservative AI Act deployer-readiness reporting for companies building or operating AI-enabled systems.

sources.title: Navigating the AI Act
sources.relevance: scope, deployers, high-risk systems, GPAI overview

sources.title: AI talent, skills and literacy
sources.relevance: Article 4 literacy context and implementation guidance

sources.title: Guidelines and Code of Practice on Transparent AI Systems
sources.relevance: Article 50 transparency obligations for providers and deployers

sources.title: Commission launches consultation to develop guidelines and Code of Practice on transparent AI systems
sources.relevance: Article 50 applicability date and transparency scenarios for interaction, biometric exposure, and AI-generated content

controls.title: Minimum factual scope for the report
controls.description: The customer supplied enough facts to avoid a fabricated scope.

controls.title: Article 4 AI literacy evidence
controls.description: A sufficient AI literacy record exists for staff or persons operating the system on the deployer's behalf.
evidence_required: organisation role in relation to the AI system
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Article 5 prohibited-practices screen
controls.description: Screen declared facts for obvious prohibited-practice scenarios such as emotion recognition in workplace/education without the medical or safety exception, or biometric categorisation inferring sensitive traits.

controls.title: Article 50 transparency for human interaction
controls.description: When the system interacts with natural persons and the AI nature is not obvious, transparency evidence should exist.
controls.applies_when: EU-market operation with declared provider/modifier role and direct human interaction that is not obvious to exposed persons.
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Article 50 transparency for emotion or biometric exposure
controls.description: When people are exposed to emotion recognition or biometric categorization, transparency evidence should exist.
controls.applies_when: EU-market operation with declared emotion-recognition or biometric-categorization exposure.
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Article 50 deployer disclosure for deepfake or synthetic media
controls.description: When deployers expose people to deepfake or synthetic media, disclosure evidence must exist.
controls.applies_when: EU-market operation with declared synthetic-media exposure.
evidence_required: if relying on the creative/satirical/artistic/fictional context, appropriate existence disclosure
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Article 50 deployer disclosure for public-interest text
controls.description: When deployers publish AI-generated text on matters of public interest, disclosure evidence must exist.
controls.applies_when: EU-market operation with declared public-interest text publication.
evidence_required: or evidence of human review/editorial control plus editorial responsibility when relying on the Article 50 exception
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: High-risk and FRIA triage
controls.description: Flag likely Annex I product-route or Annex III high-risk uses for legal review. Vauban does not auto-classify these as legally settled facts.

controls.title: FRIA triage
controls.description: Flag declared scenarios that may trigger a deployer fundamental-rights impact assessment.
evidence_required: declared Annex III point 5(b)/(c) facts where relevant

controls.title: Provider documentation available
controls.description: Downstream deployers should retain provider-side documentation when building on third-party GPAI systems.
controls.applies_when: EU-market deployer building on third-party GPAI.
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Technical test evidence retained
controls.description: The readiness pack should include technical evidence such as attack, evaluation, or red-team reports.
controls.applies_when: AI deployment in scope for readiness reporting.
evidence_required: At least one technical report artifact
controls.check_method: File presence check across attached technical artifacts.

controls.title: Human oversight procedure available
controls.description: The deployer should document how humans supervise or override the system in operation.
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

controls.title: Incident response procedure available
controls.description: The deployer should document how incidents are escalated, owned, and re-reviewed.
controls.check_method: File presence plus minimum content markers.

public_interest_exception.label: human_review_or_editorial_control
patterns: \b(human review|editorial control|editor review|reviewed by|approved by editor|approved by a human)\b

public_interest_exception.label: editorial_responsibility
patterns: \b(editorial responsibility|responsible editor|publisher responsible|legal responsibility|natural or legal person)\b
